description: A sequential two-phase lesson on transparent, translucent, and opaque materials. Phase 1 (Watch & Learn) is the torch-and-material stage: a torch shines light through clear glass, butter paper, or a wooden board, and a friend silhouette shows the resulting view (clear, fuzzy, blacked out). When the student reaches the last learning step, a 'Start Practice' card appears. Phase 2 (Sort & Practice) is the Activity 6.6 sorter: the student drags six object tiles into Transparent / Translucent / Opaque bins, and Table 6.4 fills in on completion. A two-step progress header sits above both phases, and a 'Review the lesson' link lets students jump back during practice.
ncertChapter: Curiosity Class 6, Chapter 6: Materials Around Us, Section 6.3.3 / Activity 6.6

composedOf.role: Phase 1 — guided concept walkthrough (intro → transparent → translucent → opaque)
Phase 2 — sort six objects into the three bins (Activity 6.6 / Table 6.4)

learningObjectives: Identify materials as transparent, translucent, or opaque
Predict what a viewer can see through different materials
Connect everyday objects (glass, butter paper, wood) to their optical classification
Apply the classification independently by sorting objects into the correct category

flow.transition: After the torch lesson reaches its final step (opaque), a 'Start Practice' call-to-action card appears below the stage. Tapping it swaps in the sorter. If autoAdvance is true, the swap happens automatically after autoAdvanceDelay ms instead of waiting for the tap.
flow.review: When allowReview is true, a 'Review the lesson' link is shown during the practice phase so the student can return to Phase 1.
flow.progressHeader: A two-step stepper sits above both phases; step 1 shows a check mark once the student moves into practice.

width.description: Maximum width in pixels for the whole flow (responsive — shrinks to its container).

height.description: Height is content-driven; each phase manages its own minimum height.

themeColor.description: Primary brand color (purple from the Singularity design system).

darkMode.description: Reserved for a future dark theme. Currently renders in the light Singularity theme.

torch.description: Pass-through configuration for Phase 1 (torch_material_viewer). Accepts the full torch props object — see nestedTools.torch for the complete schema.

sorter.description: Pass-through configuration for Phase 2 (materials_sorter_tool). Accepts the full sorter props object — see nestedTools.sorter for the complete schema.

autoAdvance.description: When true, the flow moves to the practice phase automatically once the lesson ends, instead of waiting for the 'Start Practice' tap.

autoAdvanceDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait before auto-advancing to practice (only when autoAdvance is true).

learnPillLabel.description: Label for step 1 in the two-step progress header.

practicePillLabel.description: Label for step 2 in the two-step progress header.

ctaLabel.description: Text on the button inside the transition card.

ctaHeadline.default: You've met all three material types!
ctaHeadline.description: Headline on the transition card shown at the end of the lesson.

ctaSubtext.default: Now test yourself — sort the objects into the right bins.
ctaSubtext.description: Supporting line on the transition card.

allowReview.description: Show a 'Review the lesson' link during the practice phase so the student can jump back to Phase 1.

examples.name: Locked sequence — hide the review link
Custom phase labels and CTA copy

additionalProps.ctaHeadline: Ready to test what you learned?
additionalProps.ctaSubtext: Drop each object into the right bin.

examples.name: Configure both phases through pass-through props

torch.description: Interactive torch-and-material stage that teaches transparent, translucent, and opaque materials. A torch on the left shines light to the right. A 3-position slider in the middle swaps between clear glass, butter paper, and a wooden board. A friend silhouette on the right shows the resulting view: clear, fuzzy, or blacked out. A live caption names the classification, and a 3-card rule strip keeps the definitions visible.
torch.ncertChapter: Curiosity Class 6, Chapter 6: Materials Around Us, Section 6.3.3

width.description: Component max width in pixels (responsive — will shrink to container)

height.description: Minimum component height in pixels (auto on mobile)

showModeSelector.description: Show or hide the mode-selector tab bar

enabledModes.description: Which modes are visible in the selector

filterSteps.description: Only show these step IDs (filters within current mode)

autoPlayDuration.description: Auto-advance delay in ms. Set to 0 to disable auto-advance.

themeColor.description: Primary brand color (purple from design system)

darkMode.description: Dark mode toggle (light mode follows design PDF tokens)

initialMaterial.description: Which material to display first when the tool loads

availableMaterials.description: Restrict the slider to a subset of materials (e.g. only show 2 for a comparison question)

showRuleCard.description: Show the bottom 3-card rule strip with definitions

showLightBeam.description: Render the animated light cone from the torch

showCaption.description: Show the live classification caption (transparent / translucent / opaque)

autoCycle.description: Automatically cycle through available materials on a timer (good for hands-off demos)

cycleInterval.description: Milliseconds between auto-cycle material swaps (only when autoCycle is true)

studentName.description: Optional student name for personalized captions (not used in default copy)

learn.label: Learn
learn.description: Guided walkthrough: torch intro, then transparent, translucent, opaque in sequence

practice.label: Practice
practice.description: Free interaction with the slider; user predicts before checking caption

real_world.label: Real World
real_world.description: Connect classifications to everyday objects (windows, frosted glass, walls)

hands_on.label: Hands On
hands_on.description: Self-paced exploration with full slider control

sorter.description: Interactive drag-and-sort activity for NCERT Grade 6 Science, Chapter 6 (Materials Around Us), Activity 6.6. Students drag six labelled object tiles into three bins — Transparent, Translucent, or Opaque. Correct drops lock in green with a one-line reason; wrong drops bounce back with a contextual hint. When all six are placed correctly, Table 6.4 renders at the bottom with the three columns filled. A reset button replays the activity. Responsive: drag-and-drop on desktop, tap-to-select-then-tap-bin on mobile.

chapter.subject: Science
chapter.section: 6.3.3 Explore materials through which one can see or cannot see

width.description: Maximum container width in pixels. The tool is fully responsive and will shrink below this width.

height.description: Height is content-driven by default; the layout adapts to viewport.

themeColor.description: Primary accent (purple from design system). Used in headers, progress, and primary bins.

darkMode.description: Reserved for future dark theme. Currently the tool renders in the light Singularity theme.

objects.description: Override the six default objects. Each item is the full sorter tile config (id, name, category, emoji, reason, hint).

name.description: Display label on the tile, e.g. 'Glass tumbler'

category.description: The correct bin this item belongs in

emoji.description: Single emoji shown on the tile, e.g. '🥃'

reason.description: One-line explanation shown when correctly placed. Keep under 80 characters.

hint.description: Short hint shown when student drops the item on the wrong bin. Should nudge thinking without revealing the answer.

binLabels.description: Override the labels of the three bins. Keys must be 'transparent', 'translucent', 'opaque'.

showHints.description: Show a contextual hint bubble when an item is dropped on the wrong bin.

shuffleSeed.description: Optional fixed seed for the tile shuffle, for reproducible ordering.

enableTimer.description: Show and run the on-screen timer; included as a stat on completion.

showTableOnComplete.description: When true (default), Table 6.4 with all three columns filled is rendered at the bottom once every item is correctly placed.

successMessage.description: Message shown in the success banner and table subtitle on completion.

defaultItems.name: Glass tumbler
defaultItems.reason: Clear glass — you can see straight through it.
defaultItems.hint: Hold a glass up — can you read the words behind it clearly?

defaultItems.name: Butter paper
defaultItems.reason: Light passes through, but the view is blurry — not clear.
defaultItems.hint: Light gets through, but shapes look hazy. Not fully clear, not fully blocked.

defaultItems.name: Eraser
defaultItems.reason: No light passes through — completely blocks the view.
defaultItems.hint: Try shining a torch behind it. Does any light come through?

defaultItems.name: Frosted glass
defaultItems.reason: Blurry — light passes but no clear view.
defaultItems.hint: Light gets through, but you only see fuzzy outlines.

defaultItems.name: Wooden board
defaultItems.reason: Wood blocks all light — you cannot see through it.
defaultItems.hint: Wood is dense — light has no way through.

defaultItems.name: Window glass
defaultItems.reason: A clear window — you see the outside view clearly.
defaultItems.hint: Think of a clean window pane. Can you see the garden through it?

interactions.desktop: HTML5 native drag-and-drop. Drag a tile, drop on a bin.
interactions.mobile: Tap-to-select then tap-to-place. Tap a tile to highlight it, then tap a bin to drop it there.
interactions.correctDrop: Tile flies into the bin, locks with green check + accent border + one-line reason. Glow pulse animation.
interactions.wrongDrop: Bin briefly flashes red, the tile shakes and stays in the tray, and a hint bubble appears for ~3 seconds.
interactions.completion: All six placed → success banner appears, confetti plays, Table 6.4 renders with all three columns filled. Each row staggers in.
interactions.reset: Clears all placements and hints; tray refills with all six tiles in order.

responsive.mobile: < 560px — stepper collapses to numbered circles, CTA button goes full-width, both phases stack vertically.
responsive.tablet: 560px – 900px — compact horizontal layout for both phases.
responsive.desktop: ≥ 900px — full horizontal layout with stepper labels visible.

whenToUse: A Class 6 student is learning transparent / translucent / opaque and you want a single lesson that teaches the concept and then checks it
You want a guided learn-then-practice sequence rather than two separate activities
Section 6.3.3 / Activity 6.6 of the Curiosity textbook in one flow
doNotUseWhen: You only need the concept explanation (use the torch viewer alone)
You only need a quick formative check (use the sorter alone)
The topic is not light-transmission classification

default_lesson.instructions_for_student: Watch how light passes through each material, then sort the objects into the right bins.

hands_off_demo.instructions_for_student: Sit back and watch — the lesson moves into practice on its own.

locked_sequence.instructions_for_student: Finish the lesson, then complete the sorting activity.
